feed.title: Baumwelt
feed.subtitle: Baumwelt is a landscape architecture firm located in Tacoma, Washington. We are powered by inspired individuals who seek a better world for all.

entry.title: Meeting Minutes (2/20/26)
entry.content:
Key Dates

Deliverable Date
Content Deadline March 13
Pre-launch Review March 20
Launch Date March 27

Derrick To-Do List

Here’s the remaining content I will need from you to complete the site. Feel free to deliver it in portions, or all at once. I’ll need to receive everything by March 13th to have sufficient time to incorporate it. Anything you can provide early is always welcome! :)

I have marked priority as follows:

  • 🔴 High priority
  • 🟠 Moderate priority
  • 🟡 Lower priority

Remaining Content 🔴

See the Content Templates section below for formatting guidance.

  • Projects
    • What to keep
    • What to remove
    • What to add
  • News
    • Anything beyond the two posts I’ve already included.

Contact Page 🟠

At the moment, I am imagining the following content:

  • Map w/ Location Pin (interactive <iframe>)
  • Directions
  • Parking?
  • A picture of your building or “storefront”?

If you have any particular needs / desires for the contact page, please let me know ASAP.

Founder’s Message 🟡

  • A brief self-introduction or description for the About page
  • A headshot or other self portrait

Alex To-Do List

So we can track progress, I will check these off as I complete them.

Content Templates 🔴

  • Share standard templates with Derrick for:
    • News
    • Projects

Scroll Animations 🔴

  • Scroll-based animation
    • “Drawing” a line in (cubic bezier or ease-out)
    • Appear
      • Fade-in
      • Slide-in

Projects Page 🔴

  • Search
    • Search bar
  • Filter
    • by Tags
    • by Location (e.g., “King County”)
  • Sort (use <details>)
    • by Name (A-Z) (optional)
    • by Category

Contact Page 🟠

  • Map
  • Directions
  • Parking

News 🟠

  • Remove dummy content
  • Adjust handling / style of featured images + separation from text
  • Remove meeting minutes

Favicon 🟠

  • Replace white with two-tone blue
  • Discuss visibility concern

Light-Dark Theme 🟡

  • Investigate bug
  • Fix bug on Home
  • Fix bug on all other pages

Hover Animations 🟡

  • Hover
    • Subtle Resize
    • Shadow Raise
    • Shadow Inset on Click

Call to Action 🟡

  • Visually distinguish from other page content

Content Templates

I have emailed you standard templates for creating news posts and project pages. We can review them together when we next meet, but I encourage you to use them now.

Each template file describes how to use it. They each have two key properties:

  • The content is written using the Markdown syntax
  • Front Matter defines data about the content

Markdown

All news posts and project pages will need to be written using the Markdown syntax. Jekyll (our site generator) interprets Markdown files (.md) and converts them to HTML.

While Word files can be converted to Markdown, writing files directly in Markdown is likely faster and less error-prone. Markdown is also easy to learn. The Markdown Guide is an excellent resource for learning Markdown.

I recommend using a simple text editor to author Markdown. Notepad will work, but something like Sublime Text provides syntax highlighting, making it easier to both read and write.

  • Start here: Getting Started
  • Most content can be created with the Basic Syntax.
  • More complex formatting options are outlined in the Extended Syntax

Front Matter

These templates will also include “front matter” where data about the content can be defined (e.g., title, author, tags, etc.). Standard data fields have semantic meaning for the site, including auto-populating sections of page layouts, as well as site-wide content organization. Here’s an example of how front matter is used in Jekyll.

Final Thoughts

This page is just for our use now. I will remove it from the production version (i.e. the final build). However, I think the Content Templates section could be expanded into a broader documentation of the site which you may find helpful to refer back to in the future. Documentation is out of scope at this time, but it’s something to think about.

If there’s anything you think is missing or needs clarification, feel free to email me!

-Alex
entry.summary: Summary of remaining work and key delivery dates.

entry.title: Template Post
entry.content:
This is the news post body. Enter your Markdown here.

File Name

Remove the underscore (_) from the front of the template when naming the project. The underscore tells Jekyll not to process and include the file in the site.

You may also author posts in the _drafts folder. When the post is ready to publish, move it to the _posts folder.

Every post must include the year, month, and day (i.e. YYYY-MM-DD) and a lowercase, hyphen-separated name (e.g., post-name). If no title is provided in the front matter, the name of the file (excluding the date) will be used as the title automatically. Use a hyphen (-) to separate each part of the date and title in the file name (i.e. YYYY-MM-DD-post-name).

The file extensions should always be .md to denote its use of Markdown syntax. HTML (.html) is also valid, but it will not accept Markdown syntax and must use valid HTML markup.

Front Matter

The project title is the project name. Each word should be capitalized. The author is a hyphen (-) separated first and lastname.

The featured-img is a file path. Change filename.ext to match the file name and extension.

The featured-img-alt is a short, objective description of the image for screen readers.

Tags accepts multiple single words or hyphen-separated (-) multi-word labels (e.g., habitat-restoration). You can use and create as many new tags as you need. Enclosing brackets ([ ]) are optional, however if you use them, you must separate each entry with a comma (,). Here they are used to visually distinguish the tag array, which is a series of entries.

SEO

Along with the post title, these front matter categories are used by a Jekyll plugin to provide search engines and link sharing with descriptive content about the post.

Image is an optional field. It can be the same file as the featured image, or a different image optimized for link thumbnails.

Finally, excerpt is a very short introductory description of the project. It displays with each project listed on the News page. If left blank, it will use the first paragraph in the post body.

General authoring guidelines

The title is a top-level heading (#). No heading in the body should be higher than second-level (##). Avoid using headings deeper than the fourth-level (####).

If you choose not to fill in a front matter field (esp. featured-img and featured-img-alt) it is best to remove any placeholder content and leave the field blank.
entry.summary: This should be a 1-2 sentence introductory statement about the project.

entry.title: Introducing Our New Logo
entry.content:
As part of our recent rebrand, we’re excited to unveil a new logo that captures the spirit and vision of our practice. At its core, the circular form suggests a horizon—a rising or setting sun—drawing the eye inward and evoking a sense of place and atmosphere. The blue palette nods to the “blue planet,” grounding our work in environmental awareness and global context.

The abstract form can also be read as a tree in plan view, merging natural systems with design thinking. A surrounding orbital band hints at movement and unity—representing the interconnectedness of landscape, ecology, and people.

In another light, the logo is seen as a creative “big bang”—a burst of energy symbolizing growth, imagination, and the powerful momentum behind every landscape we shape.
entry.summary: We are excited to unveil our new logo capturing the spirit and vision of our practice.

entry.title: PenMet Parks Minigolf Update
entry.content:
We’re thrilled to share a sneak peek of the first shipment of themed features arriving for the new minigolf course at PenMet Parks headquarters. This renovation embraces the spirit of the Pacific Northwest—including its landscapes, aquatic ecosystems, maritime culture, and folklore—with playful, hand-crafted elements designed to delight visitors of all ages.

Loaded up and ready for installation, the truck carries an exciting cast of characters and landmarks: two orcas, snow-capped mountains, a lighthouse, a ferry, a steam donkey, seals, salmon, and yes—even Bigfoot.

Stay tuned for more behind-the-scenes updates as this one-of-a-kind course comes to life!
entry.summary: The first shipment of site features has arrived at the soon to be opening minigolf course.

entry.title: Spring Update from the Field
entry.content:
As spring takes hold in the Pacific Northwest, a new season of landscape design is underway at a private lakeside villa in Pierce County. A centerpiece of the project—a mature olive tree sourced from a century-old orchard in Northern California—continues to thrive after its careful relocation last year.

Personally selected by the owner for its character and provenance, the tree was studied in detail to ensure it could adapt to the Northwest’s unique climate. Now settled into its new home, it reinforces the project’s vision of evoking the timeless feel of the Italian countryside.
entry.summary: A new season of landscape design is underway at this lakeside villa.
